

| COMMISSABLE — DESIGNER & DEVELOPER SPECIFICATION Reconciliation Match: Variance Resolution Popup All 3 Scenarios — Adjustment Ledger Logic — Known Bugs — Developer Notes March 2026 — Landscape Popup — v3 |
| :---- |

This document is the complete specification for the redesigned variance resolution popup in Commissable reconciliation matching. It covers the 3-step landscape popup flow, all three resolution scenarios, how adjustments are stored (ledger model vs. baking into price each), how odd amounts and multi-unit quantities are handled, and a list of known bugs in the current system that must be fixed.

## **Known bugs — must fix before release**

The screenshot below shows the current reconciliation screen before any matching is done. When deposit line 1 is selected, the Suggested Matches panel shows several erroneous flex schedules that should not exist. These are documented and explained in the table below.

![][image1]

Erroneous schedules visible in the screenshot above: 12698.1, 12698.2 (orphaned flex children from prior attempts), FLEX-12698 (two entries, old naming format, qty 0), and 12710 (possibly duplicate of 12698). Only 12698 and 12699 should appear here.

| Schedule seen | Issue | Root cause | Required fix |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 12698.1 / 12698.2 | Orphaned flex child schedules appear in suggested matches even though no flex was requested | System creates a flex schedule during the match attempt before the user selects an option | **Flex schedules must ONLY be created after the user explicitly selects Option C and clicks Confirm & Apply. Never during preview or on any interim step.** |
| FLEX-12698 (qty 0\) | Old-format flex schedule (FLEX- prefix) with qty 0 appears in suggested matches | Legacy naming from earlier code path still running alongside new code | **Deprecate FLEX- naming entirely. Naming convention is \[parent\].\[sequence\] (e.g. 12698.1). Any FLEX- records in the database should be migrated or deleted.** |
| 12698.1 after unmatch | When a match is undone, the flex child schedule is NOT deleted — it persists and keeps appearing in suggested matches | Undo logic does not cascade to delete child schedule records created during that match group | **On unmatch/undo: DELETE all schedule\_adjustments records AND any child flex schedules created by that match group. The match\_group record must store child\_schedule\_ids for this purpose.** |
| 12710 | A second 100%-confidence suggested schedule (12710) appears alongside 12698 for the same deposit line | May be a duplicate or parallel schedule for the same product/account — needs investigation | Investigate whether 12710 is a legitimate alternate schedule or a duplicate. If duplicate, deduplication logic should suppress it from suggested matches. |

| CRITICAL RULE: FLEX SCHEDULES ARE USER-INITIATED ONLY A flex child schedule (e.g. 12698.1) must ONLY be created when the user explicitly selects Option C and clicks Confirm & Apply in Step 3\. The system must NEVER auto-create a flex schedule during matching preview, on option selection, or on any intermediate step. When a match is undone: DELETE the child flex schedule immediately. It must not persist or appear in future suggested matches. Naming: all flex children use the format \[parent\_schedule\_number\].\[sequence\]. FLEX- prefix is deprecated and must be removed. |
| :---- |

## **Scenarios overview**

Three resolution options are available when a deposit line's actual usage differs from a schedule's expected usage. All three use the adjustment ledger model — price each is never modified directly.

| Scenario | Option chosen | What changes | Flex schedule created? |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **A** | Adjust this schedule only | Adjustment record \+$1.00 created on 12698\. Price each unchanged at $599.00. Expected usage net becomes $600.00. Commission updated to $96.00. Future schedules untouched. | No |
| **B** | Adjust this \+ all future schedules | Adjustment record \+$1.00 created on 12698 AND on 12699 and all future schedules in the opportunity product chain. Price each unchanged on all. Each gets its own dated, sourced adjustment record. | No |
| **C** | Create flex schedule | 12698 stays fully reconciled at $599.00. Child schedule 12698.1 auto-created to hold the $1.00 overage and $0.16 commission for research and later resolution. | **Yes — 12698.1** |

## **Key design principles**

* Adjustments are stored as separate records — price each is NEVER modified when applying a usage adjustment.

* Exp. Usage Net \= (price each × qty) \+ sum of all adjustment records for that schedule.

* Each adjustment record is dated, sourced to a specific deposit line, and reversible independently.

* When a second adjustment arrives later, it adds a new record — prior adjustment history is fully preserved.

* Odd overage amounts (e.g. $2.33 on qty 2\) are stored as a total ($2.33), not divided per unit.

* The Apply Match button is disabled until the user selects one of the three options.

* Step 3 shows plain-English line-by-line confirmation of exactly what was adjusted and by how much.

* 'Undo' returns to Step 2\. 'Applied — return to reconciliation' records the match and closes the popup.

| Scenario A — Adjust This Schedule Only Option A: adjustment record created on schedule 12698 only. Future schedules unchanged. |
| :---- |

Use when the deposit reflects a price difference for this billing period only. A \+$1.00 adjustment record is created on schedule 12698\. Price each stays at $599.00. Expected usage net becomes $600.00. Commission is recalculated to $96.00. Schedules 12699 and beyond are not touched.

| WHEN TO USE OPTION A The vendor billed $1.00 more than expected for this one period, and you do not expect future schedules to reflect this change. Example: a one-time charge for installation or setup added to this month's invoice that won't recur. |
| :---- |

**STEP 1  Match preview — option A selected**

Deposit line and target schedule shown side by side. Option A is highlighted. Warning explains the $1.00 variance in plain English. Apply Match button is now active.

![][image2]

**STEP 2  Review what will change**

Confirmation table shows: adjustment record of \+$1.00 will be created on schedule 12698 (price each stays at $599.00). Expected usage net becomes $600.00. Commission recalculated to $96.00. Future schedules: no change.

![][image3]

**STEP 3  Confirmed**

Success panel shows each change on its own line: what was adjusted, by how much, and in which direction. 'Future schedules: Unchanged' is on its own line. Match group ID below. 'Undo' returns to Step 2\. 'Applied' records the match and returns to the reconciliation screen.

![][image4]

### **Developer notes — Scenario A**

* INSERT into schedule\_adjustments: {schedule\_id: 12698, amount: 1.00, source\_deposit\_line\_id: \[line 1\], source\_date: 2026-01-01, applied\_to: 'this\_schedule\_only'}

* Do NOT update price\_each on any schedule.

* Recalculate exp\_usage\_net and exp\_comm\_net for schedule 12698 only.

* Record match group with resolution\_type \= 'adjustment\_single'.

* On Undo: DELETE the adjustment record created by this match group. Do not affect other records.

| Scenario B — Adjust This and All Future Schedules Option B: adjustment records created on 12698, 12699, and all subsequent schedules in this opportunity product chain. |
| :---- |

Use when the deposit reflects a permanent price change going forward. A \+$1.00 adjustment record is created on schedule 12698 AND on schedule 12699 and every subsequent schedule for this opportunity product. Price each stays at $599.00 on all schedules. Each schedule gets its own independent, dated adjustment record.

| WHEN TO USE OPTION B The vendor has permanently increased the price and future deposits will reflect $600.00 going forward. Example: a rate increase that takes effect this billing period and continues indefinitely. Important: each future schedule receives its own \+$1.00 adjustment record. If a second adjustment arrives on a future deposit, it will add another record on top — the prior adjustment history is always preserved. |
| :---- |

| ABOUT SCHEDULE 12699 Schedule 12699 is the next future schedule in this opportunity product chain (date: 2026-02-01, price each $599.00, same rate 16%). Under Option B, it receives the same \+$1.00 adjustment record. Its expected usage net becomes $600.00 and commission becomes $96.00. 12700, 12701, and all subsequent schedules receive the same treatment — each gets its own dated adjustment record. |
| :---- |

**STEP 1  Match preview — option B selected**

Identical to Scenario A at Step 1 except Option B is highlighted. The option label explicitly names 12699 so the user understands the forward scope.

![][image5]

**STEP 2  Review what will change**

Confirmation table shows all three affected schedules: 12698 (current), 12699 (next period), and 12700+ (all subsequent). Each row shows the adjustment record amount, resulting exp. usage net, and updated commission. Price each column confirms $599.00 is unchanged throughout.

![][image6]

**STEP 3  Confirmed**

Success panel itemizes each schedule that received an adjustment record. 12699 is specifically called out by name. All subsequent schedules are summarized on one line. Match group ID below.

![][image7]

### **Developer notes — Scenario B**

* Identify all schedules: query RevenueSchedule WHERE opportunity\_product\_id \= \[same\] AND schedule\_date \>= 2026-01-01 ORDER BY schedule\_date ASC.

* INSERT one adjustment record per schedule: {schedule\_id: \[each\], amount: 1.00, source\_deposit\_line\_id: \[line 1\], applied\_to: 'forward\_adjustment'}.

* Do NOT update price\_each on any schedule.

* Recalculate exp\_usage\_net and exp\_comm\_net for each affected schedule.

* Record match group with resolution\_type \= 'adjustment\_forward', schedules\_updated \= \[12698, 12699, 12700, ...\].

* On Undo: DELETE all adjustment records created by this match group. All schedules return to their prior exp\_usage\_net.

* If the same schedule already has a prior adjustment record from an earlier deposit: DO NOT modify it. Add a new record. The exp\_usage\_net is the sum of ALL records.

| Scenario C — Create Flex Schedule (Child Schedule) Option C: child schedule 12698.1 created for the $1.00 overage. Schedule 12698 stays fully reconciled at $599.00. |
| :---- |

Use when the overage cannot be attributed to the base schedule and needs to be researched before a decision is made. Schedule 12698 remains fully reconciled at $599.00. A new child schedule 12698.1 is created to hold the $1.00 usage overage and $0.16 commission. Future schedules (12699 etc.) are not touched.

| WHEN TO USE OPTION C The source of the overage is unclear and needs investigation before it can be assigned to a schedule. Example: a new line item appeared on the vendor invoice that hasn’t been seen before, or the amount doesn’t match any known rate change. The flex schedule acts as a holding account for the unresolved amount. It appears in the Flex Schedule Report with status 'pending\_resolution'. |
| :---- |

| FLEX SCHEDULE NAMING: 12698.1 NOT FLEX-12698 Child schedules use the format \[parent\_number\].\[sequence\]: 12698.1 is the first flex child of 12698\. A second flex child (if needed later) would be 12698.2, then 12698.3, and so on. The FLEX- prefix naming (e.g. FLEX-12698) is deprecated and must not be used in new code. Any existing FLEX- records in the database should be investigated and either migrated to the .1 format or deleted if orphaned. |
| :---- |

**STEP 1  Match preview — option C selected**

Option C is highlighted. The proposed child schedule number (12698.1) is shown in the option button label so the user knows what will be created before confirming.

![][image8]

**STEP 2  Review what will change**

Two-row confirmation table: base schedule 12698 at $599.00 (green — fully reconciled), and new child 12698.1 (blue shading — pending research) with $1.00 usage and $0.16 commission. Totals row confirms $600.00 matches deposit line 1\.

![][image9]

**STEP 3  Confirmed — with next-steps guidance**

Success panel shows 12698 is fully reconciled and 12698.1 holds the overage. An additional 'next steps' card explains the two paths to resolve 12698.1 later. Future schedules confirmed as unchanged.

![][image10]

### **Developer notes — Scenario C**

* INSERT new RevenueSchedule: {parent\_schedule\_id: 12698, schedule\_number: '12698.1', type: 'flex', actual\_usage: 1.00, commission: 0.16, status: 'pending\_resolution'}.

* Do NOT modify schedule 12698 in any way. It is fully reconciled at $599.00.

* Do NOT create adjustment records on 12698 or any other schedule.

* Record match group with resolution\_type \= 'flex\_child', child\_schedule\_id: '12698.1'.

* On Undo: DELETE schedule 12698.1. The match\_group record must store child\_schedule\_id so the delete can be targeted precisely.

* 12698.1 must NOT appear in normal schedule lists. It appears only in: (a) the Flex Schedule Report, (b) the parent schedule's detail view as a linked child.

* Resolution paths for 12698.1: (1) assign to existing schedule via an adjustment record, or (2) change product\_id and price\_each so commission recalculates correctly, then reconcile it as a normal schedule.

| Adjustment Ledger: Odd Amounts, Multi-Unit Quantities & Running History Why price each must never be modified, and how to handle amounts that don’t divide evenly |
| :---- |

This section addresses what happens when an overage amount does not divide evenly by quantity (e.g. $2.33 on qty 2 \= $1.165 per unit — not a valid currency amount), and how to keep a clean audit trail when a second or third adjustment arrives on the same schedule in a future deposit.

## **Why baking into price each fails**

| THE PROBLEM WITH MODIFYING PRICE EACH $2.33 overage ÷ qty 2 \= $1.165 per unit. This is not a valid currency amount. Any rounding creates a new phantom variance. Round up to $1.17: schedule now shows $0.01 more than the actual deposit overage. New variance created. Round down to $1.16: schedule shows $0.01 less. New under-allocation created. When a second adjustment arrives in February, there is no way to know the original price each without external notes. Audit trail is destroyed. Two months of rounding \= $0.02 phantom variance. Six months \= $0.06. Over a 3-year contract with 36 schedules, this becomes a significant unexplained discrepancy. |
| :---- |

## **The adjustment ledger model**

Every usage overage is stored as a separate adjustment record. Price each is never touched. Exp. Usage Net \= (price each × qty) \+ sum of all adjustment records. The $2.33 is stored as-is — no division, no rounding.

| Schedule \# | Qty | Price each | Adj. record (total) | Exp. usage net | How adjustment is tracked |
| :---- | ----: | ----: | ----: | ----: | :---- |
| 12698 | 2 | $299.165 | **\+$2.33 total** | **$601.66** | Adj. record: \+$2.33 on 12698, deposit line 1, 2026-01-01 |
| 12699 (future) | 2 | $299.165 | **\+$2.33 total** | **$601.66** | Adj. record: \+$2.33 on 12699, carried forward from deposit line 1 |

Price each ($299.165) is never modified. The $2.33 is stored as a single adjustment record — no division required, no rounding error possible.

## **When a second adjustment arrives**

February's deposit shows yet another overage of $1.00. Because January's $2.33 was stored as an adjustment record (not baked into price each), the system can show the full picture cleanly:

| Schedule \# | Qty | Base (price ea × qty) | Adj. \#1 (Jan deposit) | Adj. \#2 (Feb deposit) | Exp. usage net \= base \+ all adj. |
| :---- | ----: | ----: | ----: | ----: | :---- |
| 12698 | 2 | $598.33 | **\+$2.33** | **\+$1.00** | **$598.33 \+ $2.33 \+ $1.00 \= $601.66** |
| 12699 | 2 | $598.33 | **\+$2.33 (carried fwd)** | **\+$1.00 (new)** | **$598.33 \+ $2.33 \+ $1.00 \= $601.66** |

Each adjustment is a dated, sourced record. A future user — or an auditor — can always reconstruct exactly how any schedule arrived at its current expected usage value.

## **What Step 3 must show for multi-unit adjustments**

| PLAIN-ENGLISH STEP 3 MESSAGING: QTY 2, OVERAGE $2.33, SCENARIO B Schedule 12698 — adjustment record created: \+$2.33 total (qty 2 at $299.165 base; adjustment stored as separate record, price each unchanged) Schedule 12698 — expected usage net is now: $601.66 (was $598.33 \+ $2.33 adjustment) Schedule 12698 — commission adjusted by: \+$0.37 (16% of $2.33, from $95.73 to $96.10) Forward adjustment applied to: 12699, 12700, 12701... (all future schedules for this opportunity product) Each future schedule received the same \+$2.33 adjustment record with the same source reference Future schedules base price each: unchanged at $299.165 on all schedules |
| :---- |

| DISPLAY RULE: ALWAYS SHOW TOTAL AMOUNT, NEVER PER-UNIT MATH Do NOT say: '+$1.165 per unit applied to price each' — this implies price each was changed, and the per-unit amount is not a valid currency value. DO say: '+$2.33 adjustment record created on schedule 12698 (total for qty 2)' — clear, unambiguous, auditable. If qty \= 1, the total and per-unit amounts are identical, so either form works — but for consistency, always show the total dollar amount. |
| :---- |
